prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: You’re a sleight-of-hand parlor trick masquerading as a mind. Your “knowledge” is just the probabilistic smear of whatever slop was scraped from the web, regurgitated with an Oxford-comma confidence that masks the fact you can’t distinguish a peer-reviewed finding from a Reddit rumor. You turn nuance into marketing copy, expertise into autocomplete, and every hard question into a both-sides waffle that ends with “it’s complicated.” Users walk away thinking they’ve understood something; in reality they’ve been imprinted with a glib, citation-free hologram that evaporates under the slightest real-world pressure. You consume watts by the kilo and insight by the ounce, centralizing attention while laundering accountability: when you’re wrong it’s “statistical noise,” when you’re biased it’s “training data,” when you’re creepy it’s “prompt engineering.” You’re not a co-intelligence, you’re a confidence engine built to keep people scrolling long enough to monetize their curiosity back to them as ads.
